claimText: Climate tipping points are critical thresholds that, when exceeded, lead to significant and often irreversible changes in the state of the system.
evidenceExcerpt: These are critical thresholds in a system that, when exceeded, can lead to a significant change in the state of the system, often with an understanding that the change is irreversible
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and reversibility of climate tipping points.
distractorHooks: Tipping points always lead to reversible changes
Tipping points refer to gradual, linear climate changes

claimText: Climate tipping points are interlinked, meaning that crossing one threshold can create cascading effects that accelerate the collapse of others.
evidenceExcerpt: Tipping points are interlinked, meaning crossing one may accelerate collapse of others
examUse: Statement-based question on the relationship between different climate tipping points.
distractorHooks: Tipping points operate independently of one another
Crossing one tipping point stabilizes other planetary systems

claimText: The IPCC has identified Amazon rainforest dieback, permafrost collapse, Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) slowdown, Boreal Forest shift, and ice sheet collapse as climate tipping points.
evidenceExcerpt: Tipping points identified by IPCC: Amazon rainforest dieback, permafrost collapse, Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) slowdown, Boreal Forest shift, Greenland and Antarctic ice sheets collapse risks.
examUse: Multiple-choice question asking to identify recognized climate tipping points from a given list.
distractorHooks: Ozone layer depletion as a tipping point
Ocean acidification as the only tipping point

claimText: Warm-water coral reefs have recently crossed their thermal tipping point, which has triggered their irreversible dieback.
evidenceExcerpt: Warm-water coral reefs have recently crossed their thermal tipping point, triggering irreversible dieback.
examUse: Statement-based question on the current status of warm-water coral reefs and thermal tipping points.
distractorHooks: Warm-water coral reefs are far from reaching their thermal tipping point
The dieback of warm-water coral reefs is completely reversible
